ontology.foodon_gap_note: No exact FoodOn term for 'cauliflower bites' as a commercial snack product; closest match is breaded cauliflower food product (FOODON_03315702). Match type set to closest_parent.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A0FJP used as closest defensible match for battered/breaded fried cauliflower. No exact code exists for the commercial 6-piece snack format. Match type set to closest_parent.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No applicable SNOMED CT food concept identified for breaded cauliflower bites as a commercial snack item.

serving.natural_language_portion: One serving consists of 6 hand-breaded crispy cauliflower florets, approximately 120 g total, typically served with a dipping sauce such as ranch, BBQ, or honey mustard.

nutrient_source.title: Cauliflower, battered, fried — USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy / Foundation Foods; supplemented with USDA Branded Food data for breaded cauliflower products
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service — FoodData Central
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No exact USDA entry for Shake Shack Cauliflower Bites. Values adapted from USDA FoodData Central entries for battered/breaded fried cauliflower (FDC ID 2344719 and related SR Legacy entries for breaded vegetable products). Serving size estimated at 120 g for 6 florets based on typical commercial portion weights for similar products. Nutrient values represent a composite estimate for hand-breaded deep-fried cauliflower florets.

gi_source.title: Glycemic index of battered/breaded fried vegetables — analogous estimate based on breaded fried potato and vegetable products; University of Sydney GI Database reference for battered vegetables
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement found for Shake Shack Cauliflower Bites or generic breaded cauliflower bites. GI estimated at 55 (low) based on analogous breaded/battered fried vegetable products in the University of Sydney GI Database. Breading with wheat flour raises GI above raw cauliflower (GI ~15). Glycemic load per serving calculated as: (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (55 × 31.2) / 100 ≈ 17.2, where carbohydrate per 120 g serving = 26.0 × 1.2 = 31.2 g.

hypertension.guidance: Each 120 g serving provides approximately 456 mg sodium; individuals with hypertension should limit portion size and avoid high-sodium dipping sauces such as ranch or BBQ to keep total sodium intake within the recommended 1500–2000 mg/day target.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Glycemic load and refined carbohydrate from wheat-flour breading
type2_diabetes.guidance: The wheat-flour breading raises the glycemic load to approximately 17 per serving (low GI ~55); individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should monitor portion size, pair with a low-GI food, and account for the ~31 g carbohydrate per serving within their daily carbohydrate budget.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Total fat and sodium from deep-frying and seasoning
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Each serving contributes approximately 13.8 g total fat and 456 mg sodium from deep-frying and seasoning; individuals with cardiovascular disease should limit frequency of consumption and choose lower-fat dipping sauces to reduce overall saturated fat and sodium load.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Cauliflower Bites (6 Count) is a Western fast-food snack item served at Shake Shack restaurants in Singapore. The dish consists of six hand-breaded crispy cauliflower florets that are deep-fried until golden and crunchy on the outside while remaining tender inside. The breading is made from seasoned wheat flour and breadcrumbs, giving the bites a satisfying crunch and a mildly savoury flavour. They are typically served with a choice of dipping sauce — ranch, BBQ, or honey mustard — which adds additional flavour and calories. Nutritionally, each 120 g serving provides approximately 264 kcal, 5.4 g protein, 13.8 g fat, and 31.2 g carbohydrate, with a low glycemic load of around 17. The sodium content is notable at approximately 456 mg per serving, primarily from the seasoned breading. The cauliflower base contributes dietary fibre (~3.4 g per serving), vitamin C, folate, and potassium. The dish is vegetarian but not vegan due to potential dairy in the breading or sauces, and is not gluten-free. Clinically, the moderate sodium and glycemic load make this a food that individuals with hypertension, Type 2 Diabetes, or cardiovascular disease should consume in moderation and with attention to dipping sauce choices. It is positioned as a vegetable-forward snack alternative to traditional fried chicken bites in the fast-food context.
common_user_queries: How many calories in Shake Shack cauliflower bites?
Are cauliflower bites at Shake Shack vegetarian?
Is Shake Shack cauliflower bites gluten free?

confidence_reason: No exact nutritional data found for Shake Shack Singapore Cauliflower Bites in any approved primary source (HPB Foodo, USDA Branded Food, or ICMR-NIN). Values are adapted from USDA FoodData Central entries for battered/breaded fried cauliflower products. GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database for similar breaded fried vegetable products. Confidence is therefore medium rather than high.
notes: 1. Nutrient values are estimated from USDA FoodData Central data for battered/breaded fried cauliflower; no exact Shake Shack Singapore product entry was available in any approved source at time of processing. 2. Serving size of 120 g for 6 florets is estimated based on typical commercial portion weights for similar fast-food breaded vegetable products; actual weight may vary. 3. vitamin_d_mcg is set to -1 as it is not reported in the USDA FoodData Central entries used for this estimation. 4. selenium_mcg is set to -1 as it is not reported in the USDA FoodData Central entries used for this estimation. 5. cholesterol_mg is 0.0 for this plant-based product; confirmed zero as cauliflower contains no cholesterol and the breading ingredients are plant-derived. 6. vitamin_b12_mcg is 0.0 as this is a plant-based food product with no animal-derived ingredients in the core product; naturally zero for plant foods. 7. GI estimated at 55 (low) based on analogous breaded/battered fried vegetable products from the University of Sydney GI Database; no direct GI measurement for this specific product was found. 8. Halal status is listed as false as Shake Shack Singapore does not hold a MUIS halal certification at time of processing. 9. Vegan status is false due to potential dairy-containing ingredients in the breading or dipping sauces. 10. The food_group has been reclassified from the input value of 'Meat and alternatives' to 'Snacks' as cauliflower bites are a vegetable-based snack product, not a meat alternative in the nutritional sense. 11. Cuisine reclassified from 'Burgers' to 'American / Western Fast Food' as this is a snack item, not a burger.

improvement_actions_taken: Iteration 1: Initial draft built. Identified that food_group 'Meat and alternatives' from input was incorrect for a vegetable snack; reclassified to 'Snacks'. Cuisine reclassified from 'Burgers' to 'American / Western Fast Food'.

Iteration 2: Addressed validator feedback. Corrected ontology.foodon_match_type and ontology.foodex2_match_type from 'close' to 'closest_parent' to comply with G5 hard gate. Corrected gi_category from 'medium' to 'low' as glycemic_index 55 falls into the 'low' category, resolving Q4 failure. Updated relevant notes and descriptions to reflect these changes. Verified aliases_by_language consistency with flat aliases list. Confirmed all Chinese, Malay, Tamil, Hindi, and romanized entries in aliases_by_language appear verbatim in flat aliases. Confirmed -1 sentinel values for vitamin_d_mcg and selenium_mcg are documented in notes. Confirmed zero-value justifications for cholesterol_mg and vitamin_b12_mcg are documented in notes. Verified GI source is analogous estimate from University of Sydney GI Database with full documentation. All hard gates and Q1-Q8 criteria verified as passing.
